name: Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir - Agurain Loop
description: Cycle a moderate 28.0-mile (45.1 km) circular route around the scenic Embalse Ullíbarri-Gambo, starting from Agurain/Salvatierra.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Salvatierra / Agurain offers diverse routes across the Llanada Alavesa, a relatively flat expanse suitable for varied cycling experiences. The region is framed by the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges, featuring beech woods and providing a scenic backdrop. Routes often incorporate historical elements, such as medieval towns and megalithic monuments, alongside natural features like the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. This blend of open plains and mountain foothills provides a range of terrain for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Salvatierra / Agurain The most popular touring cycling route is Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir - Agurain Loop, a 28.0 miles (45.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route circles the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, offering pleasant views along the water. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sorginetxe and Aizkomendi Dolmens Loop, an easy 14.4 miles (23.1 km) path. This route guides cyclists past significant megalithic monuments, including the Sorginetxe and Aizkomendi dolmens. Local touring cyclists also love the Itaida Menhir – Cueva de Los Cristinos loop from Entziako partzuergoa, a 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail leading through the Entzia Mountain Range, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Salvatierra / Agurain is defined by the open plains of Llanada Alavesa, the forested slopes of the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges, and routes around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain paths. The routes in Salvatierra / Agurain are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 9 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Salvatierra / Agurain's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in the Salvatierra / Agurain area, offering a diverse range of experiences across the Llanada Alavesa and into the surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068998191/sorginetxe-and-aizkomendi-dolmens-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorginetxe and Aizkomendi Dolmens Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.4-mile (23.1 km) path that guides cyclists past significant megalithic monuments without excessive difficulty.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in this region?
A: The region offers a mix of landscapes. You'll find relatively flat expanses in the Llanada Alavesa, ideal for steady cycling, alongside the forested slopes of the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges, which feature beautiful beech woods. Routes also often incorporate natural features like the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085165966/ullibarri-gamboa-reservoir-agurain-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir - Agurain Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.0-mile (45.1 km) trail that circles the reservoir, offering pleasant views along the water.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore while cycling?
A: The area is rich in history. You can cycle past ancient megalithic monuments like the dolmen of Sorginetxe and the menhir of Itaida. The historic town of Agurain/Salvatierra itself is a highlight, with its medieval walls and churches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/837300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, a historic cave and chapel on the Camino de Santiago, is another notable point of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. The relatively flat Llanada Alavesa makes it accessible for many months, while the mountain ranges offer cooler temperatures during warmer periods. Spring brings lush greenery, and autumn showcases vibrant foliage in the beech woods.

Q: Are there any routes that offer challenging climbs or more difficult terrain?
A: Yes, for more experienced cyclists, there are challenging routes that venture into the mountain ranges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itaida Menhir – Cueva de Los Cristinos loop from Entziako partzuergoa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.3-mile (55.2 km) trail leading through the Entzia Mountain Range with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from the open plains to the scenic beech woods, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites like the megalithic monuments and medieval towns.

Q: Are there any specific natural features or viewpoints worth visiting on a cycling tour?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkorri Summit (1,523 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views of Guipúzcoa and Álava. The Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir is a beautiful spot with dedicated cycle routes. The Opakua Mountain Pass also provides incredible natural scenery where the Entzia and Urbasa ranges meet.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat along the cycling routes?
A: Salvatierra / Agurain, as a historic town, offers various amenities including accommodation and places to eat. The Great Cycle Tourist Route of the Llanada Alavesa, for example, is designed to be completed over two or three days, with Salvatierra / Agurain serving as a convenient overnight stop, indicating available services for cyclists.

Q: Is there public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to trailheads may vary, Salvatierra / Agurain is a well-established town in the Basque Country, making it generally accessible. The region's cycling network, including parts of the Great Cycle Tourist Route of the Llanada Alavesa, connects to wider areas like the Green Ring of Vitoria-Gasteiz, suggesting accessibility from larger transport hubs.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: Yes, the region is known for its greenways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1630139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a notable section of an old railway line that provides an excellent gravel track for cycling, offering a unique historical and scenic experience.
tips: The Garaio Footbridge is a structure with a length of 400 meters and a height of 30 meters, which crosses the Ullíbarri-Gambo Reservoir and offers impressive views of the reservoir and the surroundings.
A quiet place where you can take a break.

The Galarreta church is an old temple that has suffered the loss of part of its structure over the years. In the middle of the 20th century, it suffered significant damage and in 2014 its tower collapsed. However, the church has been rebuilt, revealing a double portal, Romanesque and Renaissance.

Picturesque medieval town located in the province of Álava. Its cobbled streets, churches and ancient walls is like going back in time. Highly recommend taking a bike ride through its streets to immerse yourself in the history and culture of the region. It is also an incredible place to enjoy its delicious cuisine.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse Ullíbarri-Gambo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features low difficulty, making it suitable for various levels of touring cyclists. Surfaces are mostly well-paved, though some segments might be unpaved, gravel, or dirt tracks. You may encounter gentle climbs, and in a few areas, rougher, rocky sections where dismounting could be necessary.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route from Agurain/Salvatierra?
faqs.answer: While the tour starts from Agurain/Salvatierra, many cyclists find excellent facilities, including parking, at Garaio Park. This area is well-equipped and a popular starting point for exploring the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse Ullíbarri-Gambo circular cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered family-friendly and suitable for beginners, especially the southern route between Garaio and Azua, which is about 13 kilometers long with minimal elevation gain. The overall route has a low difficulty, making it accessible for varying fitness levels.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route around the Ullíbarri-Gambo reservoir?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy continuous views of the reservoir and its natural landscapes. Highlights include the Garaio Footbridge, the Bench by the Reservoir, and the Iglesia de Galarreta. The Mendixur Ornithological Park is also along the route, offering excellent birdwatching opportunities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly vibrant, with rich red and amber foliage around the reservoir. However, the trail offers beautiful natural landscapes throughout the warmer months, making spring and summer also excellent choices for cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle around the Ullíbarri-Gambo Reservoir?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the cycling trails around the Embalse Ullíbarri-Gambo. It's a protected wetland, so please respect the natural environment and any local regulations.

faqs.question: Is the Ullíbarri-Gambo Reservoir cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the area, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through sensitive natural areas like the Mendixur Ornithological Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths, including sections of the Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior, the Vuelta completa al embalse Ullibarri-Gamboa, and the Asturian Way.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or bike rentals available along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, particularly around Garaio Park, you'll find good infrastructure including restrooms and even bike rental facilities, enhancing your touring experience.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while cycling around the reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Mendixur Ornithological Park, located along the route, is an internationally important wetland and a resting place for migratory birds. You can expect to see a variety of bird species, especially during migration seasons.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Embalse Ullíbarri-Gambo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, which is over 45 kilometers, typically takes around 2 hours and 42 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.
